config.title: Berlin AB or ABC Ticket — FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for tourists choosing Berlin public transport fare zones.

items.q: Do tourists usually need a Berlin AB or ABC ticket?
items.a: Most tourists need **Berlin AB** for normal city sightseeing. Choose **Berlin ABC** when your route includes BER Airport, Potsdam, Oranienburg, Sachsenhausen Memorial or another zone C destination.

items.q: Is BER Airport in Berlin AB or ABC?
items.a: BER Airport is in **fare zone C**, so a trip between central Berlin and BER usually needs a Berlin **ABC** ticket.

items.q: Is Potsdam included in a Berlin ABC ticket?
items.a: Yes. Potsdam is in zone C, so a Berlin **ABC** ticket is usually the right public transport ticket for a Berlin to Potsdam day trip. Palace admissions are separate.

items.q: Can I add zone C to an AB ticket?
items.a: Often yes, with an **extension ticket** for the missing zone. It can fit one extra C-zone leg, but if your day includes several rides across zones, buying ABC from the start is simpler.

items.q: How much are Berlin AB and ABC tickets in 2026?
items.a: VBB lists the 2026 single ticket at **EUR 4.00 for Berlin AB** and **EUR 5.00 for Berlin ABC**. The 24-hour ticket is **EUR 11.20 for AB** and **EUR 12.90 for ABC**. Check the official app before travel because fares can change.

items.q: Does the BerlinWalk tour need an ABC ticket?
items.a: No. The BerlinWalk route is a normal **AB** city route. It starts at Alexanderplatz, stays in the historic center and lasts about **2 hours**. ABC matters for airport travel or day trips before or after the walk.
